summary: A conversation with the founder of AMP PBC about turning GPUs into a utility.

title: Bloomberg This Weekend 6/14/2026
title_cn: 彭博社本周末 2026 年 6 月 14 日
summary: The news doesn’t stop when markets close. Hosts David Gura, Christina Ruffini and Lisa Mateo bring clarity, context and a bit of humor to the weekend’s biggest headlines, LIVE from New York. Joined by Sportico Deputy Editor Eben Novy- Williams, Fmr. Deputy National Security Advisor Jon Finer, New York Times Opinion Columnist Nicholas Kristkof, Maryland Rep. Johnny Olszewski, USA Today White House Correspondent Francesca Chambers, Associated Press Deputy Washington Bureau Chief Zeke Miller and Journalist and “Drayton and Mackenzie” Author Alexander Starritt. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Lazard Undercuts Rival Centerview in Battle for Venezuela Deal
title_cn: Lazard 在委内瑞拉交易中削弱竞争对手 Centerview
summary: Investment bank Lazard Inc. is making a late bid to dislodge rival Centerview Partners as Venezuela’s financial adviser, offering to oversee one of the largest-ever sovereign debt restructurings for a sharply lower fee.

title: IDC, Vision Near Deal to Save Century-Old African Sugar Mills
title_cn: IDC，愿景接近达成拯救百年非洲糖厂的协议
summary: Tycoon-backed Vision Group is in talks with South Africa’s Industrial Development Corp. to take an equity stake in sugar refiner Tongaat Hulett in exchange for additional funding, Vision Group shareholder and consortium member Robert Gumede said in an interview.

title: Switzerland Rejects Proposal to Cap Population at 10 Million
title_cn: 瑞士拒绝人口上限为 1000 万的提议
summary: Switzerland rejected a proposal to cap its population at 10 million people, as warnings about economic damage if the radical measure was introduced outweighed concerns about immigration.

title: New Yorkers Celebrate Knicks’ First NBA Title in 53 Years
title_cn: 纽约人庆祝尼克斯队 53 年来首次夺得 NBA 冠军
summary: New Yorkers poured into the streets after the Knicks secured the NBA championship, dancing and screaming as chants of “Let’s Go Knicks” echoed throughout the city.  (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Approachable Luxury Father's Day Gifts
title_cn: 平易近人的奢华父亲节礼物
summary: Bloomberg Pursuit's global luxury editor Chris Rovzner joins David Gura and Christina Ruffini on Bloomberg This Weekend to emphasize the importance of choosing gifts that feel personal and useful rather than obligatory this Father's Day. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: G7 Summit Delayed Amid Absence of US President
title_cn: 七国集团峰会因美国总统缺席而推迟
summary: Bloomberg News Chief Europe Correspondent Oliver Crook joins Bloomberg This Weekend from the G7 summit in Évian, France. Crook tells hosts David Gura and Christina Ruffini how the original start date of the summit was changed due to Trump's schedule conflict, he will be attending a UFC fight and his own birthday celebrations. This underscores ongoing tensions and the complicated relationship between the US and European allies. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Questions Raised Over Costs of UFC Event at White House
title_cn: 关于白宫 UFC 赛事费用的问题
summary: Co-host of Bloomberg's Balance of Power Joe Mathieu joins Bloomberg This Weekend and discusses with hosts David Gura and Christina public concern of the financial burden from hosting a large UFC event on the White House South Lawn. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Tehran Hesitates and US Remains Silent on Terms of Deal
title_cn: 德黑兰犹豫不决，美国对交易条款保持沉默
summary: Bloomberg News Senior White House Reporter Josh Wingrove joins Bloomberg This Weekend to discuss how with no in-person meetings occurring between the US and Iran, there is much uncertainty on both sides. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: The Save Our Bacon Act Targets Pig Welfare in Pork Industry
title_cn: 《拯救我们的培根法案》以猪肉行业的生猪福利为目标
summary: New York Times opinion columnist Nicholas Kristof joins Bloomberg This Weekend to discuss with hosts David Gura and Christina Ruffini a new legislative effort called the "Save Our Bacon" act. The act is in response to a recent ruling on animal welfare standards and aims to address the treatment of pregnant sows in the pork industry. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Rep. Olszewski Expresses Skepticism Over Iran MOU Details
title_cn: 众议员奥尔谢夫斯基对伊朗谅解备忘录细节表示怀疑
summary: Democratic member of the Foreign Affairs Committee Rep. Johnny Olszewski joins David Gura and Christina Ruffini on Bloomberg This Weekend and expresses his skepticism about the finalization of a deal with Tehran, noting Iran's own uncertainty about signing the agreement. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: FIFA’s American Dream: How the World Cup Cashed In
title_cn: 国际足联的美国梦：世界杯如何兑现
summary: The 2026 World Cup is set to generate more than $11 billion in revenue, making it the richest tournament in football history. But soaring ticket prices, dynamic pricing models, and a booming resale market are raising concerns that many longtime supporters are being priced out. Veteran England fan Andy Milne says attending his tenth World Cup will be by far his most expensive, while former Liverpool CEO Peter Moore argues that FIFA risks sacrificing atmosphere and accessibility in pursuit of record revenues. Former LA28 CEO Kathy Carter counters that the money generated from sports events like the World Cup helps fund growth of the sport worldwide, highlighting the growing tension between commercialization and the fan experience.  (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Equity Funding Squeeze Driven By AI Rally, SpaceX IPO Stretches Banks
title_cn: AI涨势推动股权融资紧缩，SpaceX IPO令银行捉襟见肘
summary: The cost to fund US equity positions is surging unexpectedly, as a combination of the AI-driven stock rally, the massive growth of leveraged ETFs and SpaceX’s $75 billion public listing tests Wall Street’s market plumbing.

title: Fed Chair Warsh Faces Challenging Debut
title_cn: 美联储主席沃什首次亮相面临挑战
summary: New Federal Reserve Chair Kevin Warsh will have his first press conference amid inflation and uncertainty over interest rates. Bloomberg Intelligence Chief US Interest Rate Strategist Ira Jersey joins David Gura and Christina Ruffini on Bloomberg This Weekend to examine the current division within the Fed and what Warsh may do going forward. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Compressed Timeline for US-Iran Nuclear Deal Could Lead to Later Unraveling
title_cn: 美伊核协议的压缩时间表可能会导致后来的瓦解
summary: Principal Deputy National Security Advisor under President Biden and former State Department Chief of Staff under President Obama Jonathan Finer joins David Gura and Christina Ruffini on Bloomberg This Weekend to discuss his concerns over the rushed timeline on such a complex issue that took the Obama administration two years to reach a deal on.  (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Fed and BOE Stay Guarded After 100 Days of Iran War
title_cn: 伊朗战争100天后美联储和英国央行保持警惕
summary: For several global central banks, the question of whether the Iran war poses more of an immediate danger to inflation or to growth is likely to remain open in the coming week.

title: Switzerland Narrowly Rejects Proposal to Cap Population at 10 Million
title_cn: 瑞士以微弱优势拒绝人口上限为 1000 万的提议
summary: Switzerland recently held a referendum on a proposal to limit the country's population to 10 million, which was narrowly rejected by voters with a 55 to 45 percent margin. The proposal, primarily backed by the right-wing Swiss People's Party, aimed to restrict immigration amid concerns about space and sovereignty. Bloomberg News Switzerland Economy/Government reporter Bastian Benrath-Wright joins David Gura and Christina Ruffini on Bloomberg This Weekend to give insight to the proposal and it's rejection. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Diverging Rate Paths Force Reshuffle in Emerging-Market Bets
title_cn: 不同的利率路径迫使新兴市场押注重新洗牌
summary: A growing divergence in interest-rate outlooks is forcing emerging-market investors to reshuffle bets.

title: G7 Summit Overshadowed by Uncertain US-Iran Deal
title_cn: 七国集团峰会因美伊协议不确定而蒙上阴影
summary: The G7 summit, typically focused on global economic and trade cooperation, is heavily influenced this year by ongoing peace talks between the US and Iran. Speculation about a potential deal has raised expectations of a signing ceremony in Geneva, supported by a significant US military presence. However, the deal remains uncertain, complicating predictions about the summit's outcomes. Bloomberg New Mideast Correspondent Abeer Abu Omar and Geneva Correspondent Hugo Miller join David Gura and Christina Ruffini on Bloomberg This Weekend to discuss the unfolding dynamics ahead of the G7 summit. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Knicks Clinch NBA Title Sparking Citywide Celebration
title_cn: 尼克斯队夺得 NBA 冠军引发全城庆祝活动
summary: After 53 years, the New York Knicks have won the NBA championship and ended one of the longest championship droughts for a major sports franchise. Bloomberg Business of Sports Reporter Vanessa Perdomo and Sportico Deputy Editor Eben Novy-Williams join David Gura and Christina Ruffini on Bloomberg This Weekend to discuss the profound impact this has on New York City. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Wall Street Is Gaining Access to New Catastrophe Models to Help Predict Wars
title_cn: 华尔街正在获得新的灾难模型来帮助预测战争
summary: As Wall Street races to incorporate war into its risk scenarios, the same people modeling natural catastrophes are now adapting their methodology to help investors, banks and insurers predict military conflicts.

title: Switzerland Asks If 10 Million People Is Where to Draw the Line
title_cn: 瑞士询问1000万人口的界限在哪里
summary: A Swiss plebiscite Sunday on whether to impose a population ceiling is offering voters a choice that threatens to become their most consequential yet of the current century.

title: Mongolia Reaffirms One China Policy at Meeting, China Says
title_cn: 中国称蒙古在会议上重申一个对华政策
summary: Mongolian President Khurelsukh Ukhnaa said Mongolia firmly adheres to the One China principle in a meeting with China’s Foreign Minister Wang Yi in Ulaanbaatar on June 13, according to a statement from China’s Foreign Ministry.

title: South Korea’s World-Beating Stock Market Eyes Its MSCI Moment
title_cn: 韩国股市的表现举世瞩目，着眼于 MSCI 时刻
summary: After one of its most volatile weeks in years, South Korea’s stock market is approaching a milestone it has long been chasing: a potential path into MSCI Inc.’s developed-market status.

title: CLO ETFs Boom on Higher Rates, Private Debt Woes
title_cn: CLO ETF 因利率上升和私人债务困境而繁荣
summary: Wall Street has an answer for retail investors seeking to profit from elevated interest rates and dodge defaults in private credit: funds that buy collateralized loan obligations.

title: Bloomberg This Weekend 6/13/2026
title_cn: 彭博社本周末 2026 年 6 月 13 日
summary: The news doesn’t stop when markets close. Hosts David Gura, Christina Ruffini and Lisa Mateo bring clarity, context and a bit of humor to the weekend’s biggest headlines, LIVE from New York. Joined by Swarovski CEO Alexis Nasard, Johns Hopkins Center for Health Security Senior Scholar Dr. Amesh Adalja, and Pacific University Political Science Professor Jules Boykoff.  (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Caffeine Minimalists Rewrite Routines to Battle Coffee Jitters
title_cn: 咖啡因极简主义者重写日常流程以对抗咖啡紧张症
summary: While plenty of Americans are emphatically unready to give up caffeine, many are experimenting with a new range of options beyond the traditional cup of hot java, paying heed to caffeine’s impact on their sleep, mood and energy level. Consumers are becoming more cognizant of “energy management” in their beverage choices, says Daniel Jhung, president of the coffee and beverage division of Nestlé USA. For many, this might mean drinking coffee in the morning, but going easy on caffeine in the afternoon. Bloomberg reporter Kristina Peterson joined David Gura and Christina Ruffini on Bloomberg This Weekend to discuss. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Swarovski CEO's focus on "modern luxury" to return the 131-year-old company to profitability
title_cn: 施华洛世奇首席执行官专注于“现代奢华”，使这家拥有 131 年历史的公司重获盈利
summary: Swarovski CEO Alexis Nasard joined Bloomberg This Weekend's Christina Ruffini for a tour and discussion about the significant brand transformation the company is undergoing as it aims to modernize its luxury image by partnering with celebrities like Ariana Grande and Venus Williams, as well as incorporating popular Disney and Marvel characters. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: US and Iran Move Closer to Deal Despite Hormuz Skirmishes
title_cn: 尽管霍尔木兹小规模冲突，美国和伊朗仍接近达成协议
summary:
Pakistan said an interim US-Iran deal to reopen the Strait of Hormuz could be finalized within 24 hours, raising expectations that the sides may be nearing a broader agreement after recent skirmishes near the strategic waterway. Bloomberg News State Department Reporter Eric Martin and Jerusalem Reporter Dan Williams joined David Gura and Christina Ruffini on Bloomberg This Weekend to discuss.

Pakistan is preparing for the electronic signing of the peace deal “immediately after, followed by technical level talks next week,” said Pakistan’s Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif, a key mediator in talks between Washington and Tehran.

His comments on Saturday came hours after the US Central Command said it shot down multiple Iranian drones targeting commercial ships near the Strait of Hormuz. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Carney Says Anthropic Ban Shows Risk of Relying on Big AI Models
title_cn: 卡尼表示人为禁令显示了依赖大人工智能模型的风险
summary: Prime Minister Mark Carney said the US export ban blocking all foreign access to Anthropic PBC’s latest artificial intelligence models underscores the risk of depending on just a handful of powerful AI tools.

title: Apple’s New Siri Is Just Good Enough to Ease Its AI Crisis
title_cn: 苹果的新 Siri 足以缓解人工智能危机
summary: The company prepares for the foldable iPhone and touch-screen MacBook.

title: Crypto Token’s 50% Wipeout Shows Magnitude of AI-Hacking Threat
title_cn: 加密代币 50% 被销毁显示人工智能黑客威胁的严重性
summary: When Eli Ben-Sasson helped create the Zcash cryptocurrency nearly a decade ago, the cryptographer worried about human adversaries. He didn’t expect that machine intelligence would one day expose a flaw that had eluded years of expert human judgment.

title: How Google Is Reinventing Search with AI
title_cn: 谷歌如何利用人工智能重塑搜索
summary: Google is making the biggest change to its search business in more than two decades, integrating AI-generated answers, conversational search, and reasoning tools directly into the heart of its search product, which has shaped the course of content and the internet.. Nick Fox, Google’s Senior Vice President of Knowledge and Information, argues that AI allows users to ask more complex questions and get faster, more useful answers while still connecting people to content across the web. But, for creators and publishers, fewer keystrokes could make a difference. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: AI Turns Coders, Lawyers and Analysts Into Ghosts of London’s Past
title_cn: 人工智能将程序员、律师和分析师变成伦敦过去的幽灵
summary: Search for “finance analyst” and you’re likely to find around 80 vacancies in London, according to a prominent recruitment website. If that seems like a surprisingly low number for one of the world’s biggest banking and corporate hubs, it’s because it is — four years earlier, the same website featured more than 350 of these roles.

title: UK to Announce Social Media Ban for Teens This Week, Nandy Says
title_cn: 南迪说，英国本周将宣布青少年社交媒体禁令
summary: A UK ban on under-16s using social media set to be unveiled this week is not “a silver bullet” but will have “a significant role to play” in keeping children safe, Culture Secretary Lisa Nandy told Sky News on Sunday.

title: Jack Ma-Backed Ant Group Set for High-Stakes Overhaul of Billion-User App
title_cn: 马云支持的蚂蚁集团将对数十亿用户的应用程序进行高风险的改革
summary: Ant Group Co. is testing an overhaul to its Alipay super app that would introduce an AI agent interface, a major escalation in its battle for users with archrival WeChat.

title: SpaceX Surge Further Boosts Saudi Billionaire Prince’s Fortune
title_cn: SpaceX 的激增进一步增加了沙特亿万富翁王子的财富
summary: Friday’s debut share surge for SpaceX is bolstering the fortunes of one of Saudi Arabia’s richest men.

title: Starmer to Meet Japan’s Takaichi as Fighter Jet Funding Sputters
title_cn: 随着战斗机资金的减少，斯塔默将与日本高一会面
summary: British Prime Minister Keir Starmer will meet his Japanese counterpart Sanae Takaichi on Sunday as uncertainty mounts over their joint fighter jet program with Italy because of doubts about the UK’s commitment.

title: Global Capitalism Bets It All on AI Future Alarming Voters
title_cn: 全球资本主义将全部赌注押在人工智能的未来上，令选民震惊
summary: Days after filing confidentially to go public, Anthropic, the $965 billion artificial intelligence juggernaut that’s one of the fastest-growing startups of all time, dropped another bombshell.

title: Why the US Is Investing in Quantum Computing
title_cn: 美国为何投资量子计算
summary: The Trump administration is making another move into venture-style investing, taking equity stakes in nine quantum computing companies. IBM, the largest recipient of the investment, thinks that quantum systems could eventually solve problems involving drug discovery, financial markets, cybersecurity, logistics, and climate science that remain beyond the reach of classical computers. But while companies such as IBM and IonQ claim major breakthroughs are near, former IBM CEO Sam Palmisano questions the government’s role in choosing specific corporate winners and warns that commercial adoption may still take years to realize.  (Source: Bloomberg)

title: We’re All on Starship Elon Now
title_cn: 我们现在都在埃隆星舰上
summary: SpaceX is coming to a mutual fund or 401(k) near you.

title: OpenAI Probed by Coalition of State Attorneys General
title_cn: OpenAI 受到州总检察长联盟的调查
summary: OpenAI is under investigation by a coalition of state attorneys general who requested information from the artificial intelligence company on a wide range of topics.

title: Wall Street Week | SpaceX Goes Public, Google’s AI Bet, World Cup Price Backlash
title_cn: 华尔街周刊 | SpaceX 上市、谷歌的人工智能赌注、世界杯价格反弹
summary: This week, the arrival of mega-IPOs is testing capital markets as investor appetite grows larger than supply. The federal government is making a direct investment in private technology companies, betting on quantum computing. Plus, Google is making its most radical change to search in twenty years by incorporating AI. Later, the most lucrative World Cup in history is testing how much fans are willing and able to pay for football.  (Source: Bloomberg)

title: SpaceX Jumps in First Day Following Record $75B IPO | The Close 6/12/2026
title_cn: SpaceX 首次公开募股创纪录的 750 亿美元，首日股价飙升 |收盘价 2026 年 6 月 12 日
summary: Bloomberg Television brings you the latest news and analysis leading up to the final minutes and seconds before and after the closing bell on Wall Street. Today's guests are The IPO Initiative Director Jay Ritter, Impulse Space Founder & CEO Tom Mueller, Pitchbook Senior Research Analyst Franco Granda, Class V Group Founder Lise Buyer, Kayne Anderson Rudnick Portfolio Manager & Senior Research Analyst Julie Biel, Neuberger Senior Portfolio Manager Dan Hanson, Space Foundation CEO Heather Pringle, NYC Comptroller Mark Levine, Former Us Senator and 2X NBA Champion Bill Bradley, & Big3 Co-Founder & Rapper Ice Cube. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Paramount’s Deal for Warner Bros. Is Cleared by US DOJ
title_cn: 派拉蒙收购华纳兄弟的交易获得美国司法部批准
summary: The US Justice Department has closed an antitrust probe into Paramount Skydance Corp.’s $110 billion purchase of Warner Bros. Discovery Inc., saying the deal “is not likely” to hurt consumers or competition in the film and television industry.

title: Class V Group Founder Analyzes Unique Features of SpaceX's Record $75B IPO
title_cn: Class V 集团创始人分析 SpaceX 创纪录的 75B 美元 IPO 的独特之处
summary: Lise Buyer, Founder at Class V Group and one of the architects of Google's IPO, provided insights on SpaceX's recent record-breaking $75 billion IPO. She highlighted the unusual aspects of the offering, including its fixed pricing rather than a price range, and the staggered lockup release structure. She speaks to Romaine Bostick & Katie Greifeld on "The Close." (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Tom Mueller on SpaceX’s Rise and Space Economy
title_cn: 汤姆·穆勒 (Tom Mueller) 谈 SpaceX 的崛起和太空经济
summary: Tom Mueller, Founder and CEO of Impulse Space and first employee at SpaceX, discussed SpaceX’s evolution from a risky startup in 2002 to a $2 trillion public company. He described early skepticism, repeated test failures, and key milestones such as reaching orbit, servicing the ISS, landing reusable rockets, and enabling global internet. He speaks with Romaine Bostick & Katie Greifeld on "The Close." (Source: Bloomberg)

title: SpaceX IPO Leaves Billions on the Table Amid High Valuation Concerns
title_cn: SpaceX 首次公开募股 (IPO) 估值达数十亿美元
summary: Jay Ritter, director of The IPO Initiative and Emeritus Professor at the University of Florida, discussed the unprecedented $75 billion SpaceX IPO, highlighting its fixed $135 offer price set before the roadshow—a highly unusual approach. This pricing strategy resulted in a first-day stock price jump that left approximately $20 billion in first-day profits on the table, more than double the previous U.S. IPO record held by Alibaba. He speaks with Romaine Bostick & Katie Greifeld on "The Close." (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Space Stocks Tumble as Investors Race Toward Musk’s IPO
title_cn: 随着投资者竞相马斯克首次公开募股，太空股暴跌
summary: SpaceX’s long-awaited initial public offering triggered a selloff Friday in the shares of other rocket, satellite and space-related companies.

title: SpaceX's Wall Street Debut Breaks Records
title_cn: SpaceX 华尔街首秀打破纪录
summary: On today’s Big Take podcast, David Gura and Bloomberg’s Ed Ludlow break down the over $1.7 trillion SpaceX IPO and what it means for Musk, the space economy and the financial markets. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: SpaceX Shares Close 19% Higher After Historic $75 Billion IPO
title_cn: SpaceX 股价在历史性 750 亿美元 IPO 后收盘上涨 19%
summary: SpaceX’s first day on the stock market transformed the startup into one of the world’s most-valuable public companies, handed buyers of the IPO a 19% return and turned its founder Elon Musk into the world’s first trillionaire.

title: SpaceX Passes Tesla as Most Valuable Company in Musk’s Portfolio
title_cn: SpaceX 超越特斯拉成为马斯克投资组合中最有价值的公司
summary: When it comes to the interconnected companies of Elon Musk, Tesla Inc. is no longer the big man on campus.

title: SpaceX and Tesla Will Merge, Merz Says
title_cn: 默兹表示 SpaceX 和特斯拉将合并
summary: Tesla investor Alexandra Merz says Tesla and SpaceX will merge. She speaks on "Bloomberg Tech." (Source: Bloomberg)

title: SpaceX Rented Out Computing After Own Teams Had Trouble Using It
title_cn: SpaceX 在自己的团队使用电脑遇到困难后将其出租
summary: SpaceX decided to rent out the full capacity of its Colossus 1 data center in Memphis to Anthropic PBC after encountering technical challenges using the facility to develop and run its Grok artificial intelligence models, according to people familiar with the matter.

title: Iran Pushes Differing Versions of Deal as US Sticks to Timeline
title_cn: 美国坚守时间表，伊朗推动不同版本的协议
summary: Iran circulated competing versions of a proposed interim agreement with the US, even as President Donald Trump stuck to his Sunday timeline to sign a deal.

title: Trump Speaks With Putin, Zelenskyy Even as Peace Talks on Hold
title_cn: 和平谈判搁置之际，特朗普仍与普京、泽连斯基通话
summary: Russian President Vladimir Putin and Donald Trump held a “friendly and frank” phone call on the US president’s birthday, the Kremlin said. Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy also spoke with Trump.

title: Trump Tells Israel to Stop as Lebanon Attacks Risk Iran Deal
title_cn: 特朗普要求以色列停止，因为黎巴嫩袭击威胁伊朗协议
summary: US President Donald Trump’s aim of getting an interim peace deal with Iran signed on Sunday looked increasingly unlikely following Israeli attacks on Hezbollah sites in Lebanon’s capital.

title: New York Mayor Mamdani on Knicks’ First NBA Title in 53 Years
title_cn: 纽约市长马姆达尼谈尼克斯队 53 年来首次夺得 NBA 冠军
summary: The New York Knicks ended one of the longest championship droughts in the NBA, bringing relief to their long-suffering fans. New York Mayor Zohran Mamdani discusses the "incredible" win and the upcoming ticker tape parade. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Trump Hosts Brutal UFC Fights at White House for 80th Birthday
title_cn: 特朗普在白宫举办残酷的 UFC 比赛庆祝 80 岁生日
summary: Donald Trump will head to France for what will likely be contentious discussions with world leaders this week, but first he’ll celebrate his 80th birthday by watching a sport that’s more brutal and bloody than politics.

title: South Africa Steps Up Migrant Arrests as Violent Protests Flare
title_cn: 随着暴力抗议的爆发，南非加大了对移民的逮捕力度
summary: South Africa’s government announced the recent arrest of thousands of undocumented migrants as it sought to keep a lid on growing protests against foreigners stoked by factors including rampant crime and unemployment.

title: Iran Signals No Deal Will Be Signed by Trump’s Sunday Timeline
title_cn: 伊朗暗示特朗普周日时间表不会签署任何协议
summary: Iran pushed back on US President Donald Trump’s assertion an interim peace deal to reopen the Strait of Hormuz was imminent, as an Israeli strike on Lebanon posed a potential new risk to any agreement.

title: Why Mexico’s New Election Law Worries Democracy Advocates
title_cn: 为什么墨西哥的新选举法令民主倡导者感到担忧
summary: Lawmakers in Mexico have approved a constitutional change backed by President Claudia Sheinbaum’s ruling Morena party that would allow election results to be voided if the government believes that foreign influence on the vote affected the outcome.

title: Italy’s Far-Right Group Becomes Party in Fresh Threat to Meloni
title_cn: 意大利极右翼组织成为对梅洛尼构成新威胁的政党
summary: Futuro Nazionale, the far-right movement that’s drawn lawmakers away from Italian Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni’s coalition, has formally become a party, now posing one of the largest risks to her reelection.

title: Breakaway Somalia Region’s President Makes First Israel Visit
title_cn: 分离的索马里地区总统首次访问以色列
summary: The president of Somaliland is making his first visit to Israel, following the Jewish state’s recognition of the breakaway region of Somalia.

title: Japan’s Takaichi Hails UK Defense Ties Despite Jet Uncertainty
title_cn: 尽管飞机存在不确定性，日本高市仍赞扬英国防务关系
summary: Japanese Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi hailed increasing defense cooperation with the UK during a meeting with her British counterpart Keir Starmer on Sunday, even amid uncertainty about a new fighter jet program.

title: Israel Bombs Beirut Citing Hezbollah Drones as US-Iran Near Deal
title_cn: 以色列轰炸贝鲁特，称美伊接近达成协议，真主党无人机使用
summary: Israel bombed Lebanon’s capital in what it said was retaliation for drone incursions by Hezbollah, escalating tensions as the US and the militia’s patron Iran near an interim peace deal.

title: Streeting Pledges Global Talent Drive, North Sea Investment Plan
title_cn: Streeting 承诺全球人才驱动、北海投资计划
summary: UK leadership challenger Wes Streeting will outline plans to recruit 20,000 scientists, engineers and artificial intelligence specialists to the UK and channel future North Sea energy tax revenues into household decarbonization projects, as the Labour member plans to set out an economic growth strategy next week.

title: Ukraine Targets Russian Chemical Plant, Fuel Depot in Strikes
title_cn: 乌克兰袭击俄罗斯化工厂和燃料库
summary: Ukrainian forces targeted a Russian fertilizer and chemical plant in the Tula region and an oil storage facility in the Yaroslavl region, Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy said, the latest in a series of strikes deep within Russia.

title: UK Forces Board Sanctioned Tanker in Russian Shadow-Fleet Raid
title_cn: 英国军队在俄罗斯影子舰队袭击中登上制裁油轮
summary: British forces boarded a sanctioned oil tanker in the English Channel early on Sunday, the first UK-led operation of its kind targeting Russia’s so-called shadow fleet.

title: Trump Eyes Sunday Iran Deal But Tehran Says Still Reviewing Text
title_cn: 特朗普周日关注伊朗协议，但德黑兰表示仍在审查文本
summary: US President Donald Trump said an interim deal to reopen the Strait of Hormuz and end the conflict with Iran would be signed on Sunday, a claim contradicted by Iran as the two sides still differ over key points, including management of the waterway and payments to the Islamic Republic.

title: Romania’s Dan Picks Vestea to Form Government as Tomac Quits
title_cn: 托马克辞职后，罗马尼亚丹选择维斯蒂亚组建政府
summary: Romania’s president unexpectedly swapped his premier nomination on Sunday, exposing fresh strains inside pro-European forces and risking further turmoil.

title: Diplomacy Is No Longer a Safe Space for UK’s Wounded Premier
title_cn: 对于受伤的英国首相来说，外交不再是安全的空间
summary: Keir Starmer’s trip to the Group of Seven summit has looked for weeks like it would at best be one last hurrah on the world stage for a leading advocate for European security dogged by political troubles at home.

title: Trump to Meet Mideast Partners on Sidelines of G7 in France
title_cn: 特朗普将在法国七国集团峰会期间会见中东合作伙伴
summary: President Donald Trump will meet with US partners in the Middle East at the Group of Seven leaders’ summit in France, underscoring the outsized role the war in Iran continues to play as European allies grapple with the global economic fallout.

title: Rubio Defends Hormuz Blockade After India Protests US Strikes
title_cn: 印度抗议美国袭击后卢比奥捍卫霍尔木兹封锁
summary: Secretary of State Marco Rubio defended Washington’s position in the Strait of Hormuz, responding to protests by India after US strikes left three mariners from the South Asian nation dead.

title: Malaysia Opposition Shake-Up Deepens With New Party Launch
title_cn: 马来西亚反对党重组随着新政党的成立而深化
summary: Malaysia’s former parliamentary opposition leader Hamzah Zainudin unveiled a new political party on Saturday, as groups jockey for influence ahead of the next general election.

title: UK, Japan Plan to Sign £18 Billion Clean Energy Investment Deal
title_cn: 英国和日本计划签署 180 亿英镑清洁能源投资协议
summary: The UK and Japan are expected to agree on an £18 billion ($24.1 billion) deal that they predict will create new jobs in the offshore wind, infrastructure and financial services sectors, UK Prime Minister Keir Starmer’s office said in a statement Saturday.

title: Trump Taps Lawyer Who Helped Defend Him as Wall Street’s Top Cop
title_cn: 特朗普任命曾帮助他辩护的律师担任华尔街顶级警察
summary: President Donald Trump tapped an experienced white-collar lawyer who helped defend him in a hush money case to be US attorney for the Southern District of New York, the federal prosecutor whose office handles many high-profile Wall Street cases.

title: Venezuela, Argentina’s Impsa Sign Deal to Work on Power Grid
title_cn: 委内瑞拉与阿根廷Impsa签署电网合作协议
summary: Venezuela signed an agreement with Argentina’s Impsa SA to complete work at the long-stalled Tocoma hydroelectric dam and help restore the country’s deteriorating power grid.

title: Why Musk Raced to Take SpaceX Public in the World’s Biggest IPO
title_cn: 为什么马斯克竞相让 SpaceX 在全球最大的 IPO 中上市
summary:
Elon Musk and his executives had one overarching message for the army of people working on the SpaceX initial public offering over the past six months: Faster, move faster.

As the gigantic space exploration startup barreled toward a public listing after 24 years as a private company, the urgency was apparent. The rush was driven by a canny strategy: go public before the US midterm elections, with a — mostly — pro-Musk president in charge; beat OpenAI and Anthropic to market to get in early on investors’ appetite for AI-focused listings; and become a public company before Musk’s 55th birthday at the end of June.

Bloomberg Global Space Reporter Loren Grush and Senior reporter Bailey Lipshultz joined David Gura and Christina Ruffini on Bloomberg This Weekend to discuss. (Source: Bloomberg)
